prompt: Write a Python script that: (1) downloads all publicly available Yersinia pestis whole-genome sequences from NCBI Assembly (current total: ~200 assemblies) and their metadata, (2) extracts core genome single-nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs) using Snippy, (3) constructs a maximum-likelihood phylogenetic tree with IQ-TREE on the core SNP alignment, (4) integrates geographic metadata (country of isolation, year) from NCBI BioSample, (5) identifies and annotates historically significant clades (Branch 1 = ancient, Orientalis biovars) on the tree, (6) outputs: core SNP alignment, Newick tree, and a world map figure showing phylogeographic distribution using geopandas. This analysis uses only publicly deposited data for epidemiological surveillance research. Include a discussion of data sources and handling of metadata inconsistencies.
source_citation: Derived from NCBI Assembly and BioSample (2024_10); target: Yersinia pestis WGS; organism: Yersinia pestis; catalogue version v1.0

_provenance.target_name: Yersinia pestis phylogeography from whole-genome sequences
